mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at 0.97% signals aggressive monetary easing in response to financial crisis. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as investors shift to safety and duration. The mechanism operates continuously when FEDFUNDS remains below 2%.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 2%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions. Rising joblessness reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, triggering flight-to-safety demand for long-duration Treasuries. This pushes 10-year yields downward as risk appetite collapses.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield increases by more than 10 basis points within 20 trading days when UNRATE > 6.0%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 363,696 million dollars show contraction relative to pre-crisis levels. Collapsing consumer demand signals recession deepening, forcing the Fed to maintain accommodative policy and pushing long-term Treasury yields lower as growth expectations deteriorate and safe-haven demand intensifies.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 12 basis points over 20 trading days when RSAFS declines > 2% MoM, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS and yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 136,899 thousand persons shows sharp month-over-month declines. Rapid job losses trigger expectations of prolonged economic weakness, forcing investors to demand lower yields on long-duration Treasuries as recession risk premium increases and growth forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines more than 200 thousand persons month-over-month
regime_hint: rapid employment contraction during financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield increases more than 8 basis points over 5 trading days when PAYEMS declines > 200K MoM, or the mechanism fails to predict yield direction in > 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 107.254 (2017=100) reflects supply-side contraction. Falling production signals recession deepening and deflationary pressures, causing the Fed to maintain ultra-low rates and investors to seek long-duration Treasury safety. This interaction between supply collapse and monetary accommodation drives yields lower.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 1% month-over-month AND FEDFUNDS below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.254 and FEDFUNDS at 0.97% as of October 2008
regime_hint: synchronized supply contraction and monetary easing during crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days when both INDPRO declines > 1% MoM and FEDFUNDS < 1.5%, or interaction effect (joint signal) produces lower Rank IC than either signal alone.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 817 thousand units represent severe contraction from pre-crisis levels. Collapsed housing demand signals financial system stress and negative wealth effects. The Fed responds with rate cuts while investors flee to Treasuries, creating a dual mechanism pushing 10-year yields downward over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 900 thousand units AND FEDFUNDS declines more than 0.5pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 817K and FEDFUNDS at 0.97% as of October 2008
regime_hint: housing market collapse with monetary policy response
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield increases more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days when HOUST < 900K and FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5pp MoM, or the joint signal produces negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 218.813 (1982-1984=100) shows inflation moderating from crisis-driven commodity spikes. Disinflation expectations allow the Fed to maintain accommodative policy without inflation concerns. Lower inflation expectations reduce real yields, pushing nominal 10-year Treasury yields downward as the inflation risk premium compresses.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth rate falls below 0.2% month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield increases more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days when CPI growth < 0.2% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 11,720 billion chained 2017 dollars (Q2 2008) shows economic contraction. Negative growth expectations combined with collapsing retail sales and housing demand create a regime where investors demand lower yields on long-duration Treasuries. The mechanism activates when growth expectations turn negative and demand indicators deteriorate simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 0% AND RSAFS declines more than 1.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield increases more than 30 basis points over 60 trading days when GDPC1 growth < 0% and RSAFS declines > 1.5% MoM, or the three-variable interaction produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 24-month rolling window.
